commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is needed; maintainer action is to wait for required checks and explicitly accept the small-interval compatibility and availability tradeoff before merge.; Cleared: The diff is limited to heartbeat scheduling logic and colocated tests; no security or supply-chain surface changed. (sha=a061f14933f8b0ff16bb0bdca112723ce3ae8fe7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces the static heartbeat active-hours seek iteration cap with a 30-second minimum phase-aligned batch step plus backward walking, and adds scheduler regression tests for sub-minute and sub-second intervals.

PR surface: Source +26, Tests +118. Total +144 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows the helper advances by `intervalMs` but stops after 10,080 iterations, so accepted sub-minute intervals can stop before the seven-day active-hours horizon.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; maintainer action is to wait for required checks and explicitly accept the small-interval compatibility and availability tradeoff before merge.

target.title: fix(heartbeat): remove static iteration cap in seekNextActivePhaseDueMs
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where agents with sub-minute heartbeat intervals and active
hours would permanently stop running heartbeats when the current time fell
outside the active window — the static 10,080 iteration cap was calibrated
for 1-minute intervals and exhausted too early for shorter intervals.

## Why This Change Was Made

**Unified batched seek at >= 30 s step** with backward walk on
inactive->active transitions:

\`\`\`ts
const MIN_SEEK_STEP_MS = 30_000;
const multiplier = Math.max(1, Math.ceil(MIN_SEEK_STEP_MS / intervalMs));
const batchStepMs = intervalMs * multiplier;
// … backward walk on prevWasActive===false -> active transition
\`\`\`

| intervalMs | multiplier | batchStepMs | max iterations | narrow windows |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 60 s+ | 1 | ≤ 60 s | ≤ 10,080 | ✅ per-candidate |
| 30 s | 1 | 30 s | 20,160 | ✅ per-candidate |
| 59 s | 1 | 59 s | 10,251 | ✅ per-candidate |
| 1 s | 30 | 30 s | 20,160 | ✅ 30 s < min window |
| 500 ms | 60 | 30 s | 20,160 | ✅ backward walk |
| 1 ms | 30,000 | 30 s | 20,160 | ✅ backward walk |

Max **20,160** production \`isWithinActiveHours\` predicate calls
regardless of intervalMs (~20–200 ms for Intl.DateTimeFormat per call).

## User Impact

Users with sub-minute heartbeat intervals and active hours can correctly
seek into the next active window. No impact on minute-or-larger intervals
(multiplier = 1).

## Evidence

**Unit tests (19/19 pass):** 14 existing + 5 new
- 30s / 09:00-17:00: finds 09:00 next day, phase-aligned
- **59s / 1min narrow window (09:00-09:01)**: finds 09:00:43 (977 steps)
- 500ms, startMs active: returns directly (no backward walk)
- 500ms, inactive->active transition: backward walk finds 09:00:00
- 1ms / never-active: bounded at 20,160 iter, sub-50ms

**Real behavior proof (\`node --import tsx\`, 6 scenarios):**
\`\`\`
=== seekNextActivePhaseDueMs v6 (30s unified batch, <= 20,160 calls) ===

1. 30s, 09:00-17:00, start 17:00 -> 09:00:00 next day (active: true)
2. 59s, 09:00-09:01 window, start 17:00 -> 09:00:43 (steps: 977)
3. 7s, always-active, start 12:00 -> 12:00 (=startMs)
4. 1s, never-active, start 12:00 -> 12:00 (elapsed: 0.6ms)
5. computeNextHeartbeatPhaseDueMs (30s), now 10:00 -> 10:00:30
6. Full-path: 30s, 20:00->09-17 active -> 09:00:15 next day (active: true)
\`\`\`
target.author: wendy-chsy
